---
title: "LRP63: Mechanism model — letter-sound (LS) -> word reading (WR), moderated by nonword decoding (NW)"

format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    theme: cosmo
    number-sections: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-links:
      - repo
---

{{< include _partials/_header.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_setup.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_gate_badge.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_key_findings.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_reading_guide.qmd >}}

## Overview

LRP63 is a **joint-readiness** interaction model, companion to [LRP61](../lrp-rli-mech-061)
(blending) and the vocabulary-moderator family. It extends [LRP58](../lrp-rli-mech-058) — the
mechanism estimate $f^L$ of letter-sound knowledge on word reading — with a **linear moderation**
by **nonword decoding** $N$ (`nonword`, NW). The mediation suite shows the word-reading gain does
not measurably run *through* decoding (`med-074` $\mathrm{NIE} \approx +0.0$ words, 89% CrI $-0.4$
to $+0.5$); LRP63 asks instead whether the letter-sound $\to$ word-reading **conversion is gated
by** decoding — whether letter sounds only translate into word reading once nonword decoding is in
place.

Letter sounds enter as the nonparametric **HSGP** curve $f^L$ (as in LRP58); nonword decoding
enters as a standardised linear main effect plus the interaction:

$$
\eta = \dots + f^{L}(\operatorname{logit} L_{\text{post}})
       + \gamma_{\text{mod}}\, z(N)
       + \gamma_{\text{int}}\, z(\operatorname{logit} L_{\text{post}})\, z(N)
$$

where $z(\cdot)$ standardises to mean 0, sd 1 over the fitted rows. Both cross-coupling
coefficients use the regularising $\mathrm{Normal}(0, 0.3)$ prior.

_Reading the interaction._ $\gamma_{\text{int}}$ is the change in the letter-sound -> word-reading slope per +1 SD of
nonword decoding: $> 0$ = **synergy**, $\approx 0$ = **additive**, $< 0$ = **substitutive**.

## Adjustment set

The revised LRP58 $L \to W$ set (#245): $\{G, A, HS, IS(\text{attend}), SP, W_{\text{pre}}\}$;
nonword decoding $N$ enters **additionally**, via its main effect and interaction only.

_Decoding is a downstream skill, not a parallel prerequisite._ Nonword decoding
$N = \mathrm{NW}$ is a **DAG-descendant of the exposure** $L$ ($\mathrm{LS} \to \dots \to \mathrm{NW}$).
Conditioning on it makes $f^L$ a **controlled-direct** effect of letter sounds *at fixed decoding*,
and $\gamma_{\text{int}}$ is **effect-modification by a downstream skill**, not a symmetric "need
both" prerequisite test — the estimand caveat documented on [LRP72](../lrp-rli-mech-072).

_Weakly powered — suggestive at best._ Nonword decoding is $\approx 57\%$ floored (median 0), so
$z(N)$ carries little variation and this interaction is **weakly powered**; read
$\gamma_{\text{int}}$ (and the nested LOO contrast against [LRP63base](../lrp-rli-mech-163)) as an
exploratory description. It complements the descriptive finding that a small sight-word subgroup
reads with little decoding while fluent reading (40+ words) always co-occurs with decoding.

_Adjusted association, not causal._ Latent general ability is not adjusted for, so $f^L$,
$\gamma_{\text{mod}}$ and $\gamma_{\text{int}}$ are **latent-GA-confounded adjusted associations**,
never causal effects.

![Model diagram](model_graph.png){#fig-model .lightbox fig-align="left"}

{{< include _partials/_priors.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_prior_predictive.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_results_mechanism.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_technical.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_footer.qmd >}}
